table_name: List of Countries by GDP Sector Composition
schema_context:
## Table 1: List_of_Countries_by_GDP_Sector_Composition (230 rows × 15 columns)

### Description
Data loaded from CSV file: 1.csv (Dataset: List of Countries by GDP Sector Composition)

### Schema (15 fields)
  - Index Number  -- type: int64, values: 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, ..., 225, 226, 227, 228, 229
  - Rank -- type: object, values: -, 1, 10, 100, 101, ..., 95, 96, 97, 98, 99
  - Country/Economy -- type: object, values: Afghanistan, Albania, Algeria, American Samoa, Andorra, ..., West Bank, Western Sahara, Yemen, Zambia, Zimbabwe
  - GDP (millions of $) -- type: object, values: -, 10, 10070, 100900, 1028, ..., 9410, 95000, 9740, 9770, 9828
  - Agriculture GDP -- type: object, values: -, 1, 10, 101, 1010, ..., 9635, 966, 99, 991020, 992
  - Agriculture Sector GDP Rank -- type: object, values: -, 1, 10, 100, 101, ..., 95, 96, 97, 98, 99
  - Agriculture Sector GDP of % Contribution -- type: object, values: -, 0, 0.1, 0.2, 0.3, ..., 9.4, 9.6, 9.7, 9.8, 9.9
  - IndustryGDP -- type: object, values: -, 10209, 102198, 1036, 107191, ..., 96248, 97, 989, 99, 9906
  - IndustryRank -- type: object, values: -, 1, 10, 100, 101, ..., 95, 96, 97, 98, 99
  - Industry%ofGDP -- type: object, values: -, 0, 10, 10.1, 10.5, ..., 7.6, 7.7, 8.9, 9.1, 9.9
  - ServicesGDP -- type: object, values: -, 10082, 10186, 10312, 10369, ..., 94970, 968, 969794, 977170, 991
  - ServicesRank -- type: object, values: -, 1, 10, 100, 101, ..., 95, 96, 97, 98, 99
  - Services%ofGDP -- type: object, values: -, 100, 27, 28.4, 32.5, ..., 91.3, 92.7, 93.3, 93.8, 96
  - Year GDP  -- type: object, values: -, 2003, 2005, 2008, 2009, ..., 2013, 2014, 2015, 2016, 2017
  - Year Sector -- type: object, values: -, 2000, 2002, 2003, 2006, ..., 2014, 2015, 2016, 2017, FY12/13

### Sample Data (first 5 rows)

chain_of_thought.target_audience: Business executives and strategic planners in multinational corporations and investment firms
chain_of_thought.curator_intention: Encourage the audience to accept the diversity in economic structures across countries and to investigate sectoral GDP composition for market-entry or investment decisions
chain_of_thought.common_ground: Both curator and audience are interested in identifying high-potential economies by sector, uncovering which countries have dominant sectors, and determining suitable strategies by sector weight

summaries.narrative_summary: This dataset enables business leaders to compare sectoral economic strengths across countries, revealing which countries rely more on agriculture, industry, or services. By examining fields such as GDP (millions of $), Agriculture Sector GDP of % Contribution, Industry%ofGDP, and Services%ofGDP, executives can visualize regional opportunities and risks, guiding investment and expansion strategies. The structure supports cross-country benchmarking and helps establish sectoral dominance relevant for business decisions.

chain_of_thought.target_audience: Policy makers and economic development agencies at national and regional levels
chain_of_thought.curator_intention: Encourage the audience to agree that understanding sectoral contributions and GDP rank facilitates targeted economic policies and resource allocation
chain_of_thought.common_ground: Both curator and audience look to maximize societal impact by allocating resources efficiently and evaluating sector-based development progress using sector ranks and GDP percentages

summaries.narrative_summary: The schema permits policy makers to visualize and analyze the relative strength and development stage of agriculture, industry, and services sectors across various economies. Fields like Agriculture Sector GDP Rank, IndustryRank, ServicesRank, and sectoral % contribution support comparative analysis for policy formulation, allowing agencies to benchmark progress, identify underdeveloped sectors, and inform resource allocation decisions. Visualization of ranks and contributions over time fosters evidence-based policy design.

chain_of_thought.target_audience: Academic researchers and economic analysts examining global macroeconomic trends and structural shifts
chain_of_thought.curator_intention: Encourage the audience to establish deeper knowledge of historical sectoral trends, temporal shifts, and correlation between overall GDP and sectoral composition
chain_of_thought.common_ground: Curator and audience are aligned on developing longitudinal analyses, utilizing year fields alongside sectoral composition to infer causal or correlational relationships

summaries.narrative_summary: Researchers are empowered to explore global economic restructuring by leveraging fields such as Year GDP, Year Sector, and sectoral % contribution. The schema enables visualization of temporal patterns and changes in sector dominance, facilitating studies of industrialization, service sector growth, and agricultural decline. Analysts can also investigate correlations between GDP size and sector composition, yielding insights into development trajectories and structural transformation.

chain_of_thought.target_audience: Consultants and international trade advisors seeking strategic insights into sectoral export or import dependencies
chain_of_thought.curator_intention: Encourage the audience to explore how sectoral composition impacts trade opportunities and to infer potential trade partners or threats based on sector dominance
chain_of_thought.common_ground: Curator and audience are both focused on leveraging sectoral breakdowns to inform trade strategies, signify competitive advantages, and highlight inter-country complementarities

summaries.narrative_summary: This schema offers consultants and trade advisors tools to map sectoral strengths and weaknesses across countries, which can be visualized in relation to global trade patterns. By analyzing GDP (millions of $), sectoral contribution percentages, and ranks, advisors can identify economies ripe for sector-specific trade partnerships or anticipate competitors in global markets. The table's sectoral breakdown delivers actionable intelligence for shaping international trade and investment advice.
